title: nepeta podostachys subsp. paulsenii
intro: Nepeta podostachys subsp. paulsenii, also known as Paulsen's catmint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of China and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, headaches, and digestive issues, as well as its use in skincare for its antiseptic and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Nepeta podostachys subsp. paulsenii is typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
